raw_text:
Veridian Design Systems

Democratizing enterprise design through accessible, scalable digital experiences

MID PRODUCT DESIGNER

San Francisco, CA (Hybrid) · Full-time · $120,000 - $155,000 per year

Join Veridian as a Mid Product Designer and play a key role in shaping the future of our design

platform. You'll work across product teams to design intuitive, user-centered features that empower

thousands of designers daily. With 2-5 years of experience, you'll bridge the gap between complex

enterprise workflows and elegant user experiences, taking ownership of medium-complexity features

from conception through delivery. You'll collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and

research to ensure our platform remains the gold standard for design systems management.

ABOUT US

Veridian Design Systems is a SaaS platform that helps enterprises build, maintain, and evolve their

design systems at scale. We serve Fortune 500 companies and fast-growing startups alike, providing
tools and guidance for cross-functional teams to collaborate seamlessly. Our mission is to make world-

class design accessible to organizations of all sizes by removing friction from the design-to-
implementation workflow.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Design and iterate on 3-5 medium-to-high complexity product features per quarter, creating

comprehensive flows, wireframes, and high-fidelity prototypes in Figma

Conduct interaction design workshops with cross-functional stakeholders to define use cases,

edge cases, and success metrics for new features

Build and maintain components within our design system in Figma, ensuring consistency with

established design tokens and patterns

Create and document design specifications and component documentation for handoff to
engineering teams using Figma specs and design system libraries

Participate in usability testing sessions by recruiting users, conducting interviews, and
synthesizing findings to inform design iterations

Collaborate with the product management team to define feature scope, map user journeys, and
validate design solutions against product requirements

Mentor junior designers on prototyping techniques, design system best practices, and cross-
functional communication

Lead design critique sessions, providing and receiving constructive feedback to elevate the quality

and consistency of design work

 REQUIRED

2-5 years of professional product design experience, ideally at a B2B SaaS or software company

Proficiency in Figma, including component creation, variants, and design systems management

Strong portfolio demonstrating interaction design skills, with examples of end-to-end feature

designs from concept to high-fidelity

Experience creating and working with design systems, design tokens, and maintaining design

consistency across products

Solid understanding of user-centered design principles and ability to balance business

requirements with user needs

Demonstrated ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams and communicate design

rationale to non-designers

NICE TO HAVE

Experience with prototyping tools such as Framer or Protopie for advanced interaction
demonstrations

Familiarity with accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1) and experience designing inclusive products

Exposure to design research methodologies and running your own user interviews or testing

Knowledge of web development fundamentals (HTML/CSS) and familiarity with developer handoff
tools

TECH STACK

Figma (primary design tool)

  Design Systems (components, tokens, documentation)

Interaction Design principles and patterns

Prototyping (Framer/Protopie)

  User Research and Testing tools (Maze, UserTesting)

  Design Tokens and specifications

Slack (collaboration and feedback)

  Jira (workflow management)

  Loom (design documentation and walkthrough videos)

BENEFITS

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with company-paid premiums

Professional development budget of $2,500 annually for conferences, courses, and certifications

Flexible PTO policy with a minimum of 20 days per year plus holidays

Hybrid work arrangement with two office days per week in San Francisco; option for remote-first
after probation

Free Figma seat and access to all premium design tools and software

Generous parental leave (16 weeks for primary caregivers, 12 weeks for non-primary caregivers)

Quarterly design team offsites and annual all-hands company retreat

Stock options and performance bonus program (15-20% of base salary)

HOW TO APPLY

 
 Submit your resume, portfolio link (ideally on your personal site or Figma community), and a 1-2

sentence cover letter explaining why you're excited about design systems to careers@veridian-

design.com. We review applications on a rolling basis and will follow up within 5-7 business days. Initial

interviews include a portfolio walkthrough, followed by a design exercise focused on interaction design

and cross-functional collaboration.
